formations_blocks: Spain in possession, settling into a passing rhythm
Cape Verde playing with a back five
Cape Verde in a 4-4-2 defensive shape that collapses into a back line of five when the ball moves through midfield
Attack-versus-defense dynamic with Spain circulating the ball against a compact block
adjustments: Cape Verde adding another man or two to the back line as the ball progresses through midfield
Spain trying to provide width down the right through Llorente due to missing natural wide players
Cape Verde looking to exploit space in behind when they win the ball
key_matchups: Cape Verde runners versus Spain's space in behind
phases.summary: Spain start by controlling possession but lack natural width, leaving them trying to progress mainly down the right. Cape Verde defend with a deep, flexible block that becomes a back five and look to break into the space Spain leave behind, creating an early attack-versus-defense pattern with a few promising transitions in either direction.

formations_blocks: Spain controlling possession against a compact Cape Verde defensive setup
Cape Verde working in a midfield-to-wide transition phase when they regain the ball
adjustments: Spain lacking width and the usual Lamine-driven threat out wide
Cape Verde choosing to recycle possession backwards after a promising wide break
No major structural shift or substitution is described in the text
key_matchups: Ferran vs Oyarzabal with Sidny Cabral closing down the resulting touch
phases.summary: Spain enjoyed the ball and probed, but for most of this spell they struggled to turn possession into a clear chance, with the attack lacking pace and wide penetration. Cape Verde offered occasional moments in transition, but poor decision-making and Spain’s recovery kept them from sustaining pressure, leaving the game open yet still relatively controlled.

formations_blocks: Spain attacking against a compact box defense
adjustments: Spain briefly improved just before half-time by using cross-kicks from centre to wide
Spain showed no clear tactical change at half-time beyond attempting more of the same
phases.summary: Cape Verde set up in a highly organised, disciplined low block and produced an outstanding box defence, frustrating Spain throughout the first half. Spain looked lethargic and struggled to create clear openings, though they had a short spell before the break when quicker centre-to-wide switching via cross-kicks threatened to unlock the defence. The half-time picture is one of a classic rearguard action against a slow, uncertain Spain attack.

formations_blocks: Spain territorial possession and sustained pressure
adjustments: Cucurella used as the outlet, pulling wide on the left
Pedri repeatedly receives and plays first-time or carries to create angles
Pedri and Oyarzabal combine to press/win the ball higher up
key_matchups: Cucurella vs Cape Verde’s wide defensive coverage
phases.summary: Spain continue to probe a packed defensive shape, using Cucurella as a wide outlet and Pedri as the main game-breaker between and beyond the lines. The phase is defined by repeated wide-to-central combinations, high pressure on loose balls, and Cape Verde’s defenders and goalkeeper surviving a succession of close-range and aerial threats.

formations_blocks: Cape Verde defending in a well-organised low block
Spain circulating possession and sustained attacking pressure
adjustments: Cape Verde substitute Joao Paulo for Sidny Cabral, who was on a yellow and marking Lamine
Cape Verde substitute Arcanjo for Monteiro
Spain continuing to attack through wide deliveries and crossing rather than changing structure clearly
key_matchups: Lamine vs Cape Verde's right side
Cucurella arriving at the back post vs Vozinha
phases.summary: Spain dominated possession and kept probing, but Cape Verde’s compact defensive block repeatedly held firm and cleared danger. The phase was defined by substitutions, fatigue and mounting tension, yet Spain’s attacks remained too slow and lacked the numbers to break the last line consistently. Their best openings came from wide areas and crosses, but chances were still ending in routine clearances or comfortable saves.
